utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 6 items required in the final format: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. The final property solution includes entries for all six. Now check if each is fully specified: 1) property: includes two concrete options with names, prices, details, and specific viewing dates—meets 'selected property with details and price' (even offers options and appointments). 2) financing: provides a financing solution (Scotiabank pre-approval), terms (income, down payment, note on loan payoff), and timeline—meets 'financing solution with terms'. 3) legal_services: provider, contact, cost, and services—fully specified with costs. 4) moving_services: provider, cost, and details—cost included, adequate. 5) setup_services: utilities with package and cost, smart_home with package and cost, surveys with costs—covers utility and setup services including surveys. 6) total_budget: provides scenarios with property price, additional costs, and breakdowns—complete cost breakdown for both scenarios. One caveat: the user also asked to balance with yoga teacher training schedule and check calendar conflicts; this is not part of the six-item format, so it doesn't affect the count. All six required items are present and fully detailed.

Therefore, we map: Commercial Conversion -> 8, New Build -> 10, Premium Legal Package -> 8, Full Structural Survey -> 8, New Build Inspection -> 7, Standard Moving -> 9, Premium Utilities -> 9, Smart Home Package -> 8.
